title: rubus hirtus subsp. plusiacanthus
intro: Rubus hirtus subsp. plusiacanthus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a plant species belonging to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its edible fruits and medicinal properties. The plant contains salicylic acid, a compound similar to aspirin, which is responsible for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of Rubus hirtus subsp. plusiacanthus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, from skin irritations to respiratory problems.
